title: abductor: an external-oracle gate for generalizing program repair
description: abductor externalizes the test so an LLM has to represent the rule instead of tabulating the example. Hand a model a bug whose real fix is a predicate and it patches the case in front of it; told to 'be general' it agrees and stays narrow; told to 'write its own tests' it writes the tests it already believes in. abductor breaks the loop by holding an answer key the model cannot see: it enumerates a fixpoint-closed space of cases for the property under repair (wider than the model's hypothesis), calibrates each case against a known-good baseline so every case carries external ground truth, and exposes one pass/fail gate the model hill-climbs against. The gate's coverage, not the model's prior, sets the generalization frontier, and the search records itself as a replayable hypothesis graph (fixes as nodes, counterexamples as edges). The check is a disagreement, an XOR or symmetric difference between what a system believes and what is true, the check tests and type systems cannot give because absence has no test. Tooling behind the paper 'The Hypothesis Graph: Semantic Memory Written by Methodeutics'.
notes: DOI is minted on the Zenodo release; the version tag and DOI are recorded in CITATION.cff.
